presentation_metadata.title: Building Your First Agentic OS with OpenClaw

data.title: 🦞 OpenClaw and the Agentic OS era
Alfredo Morresi
bullets: Community builder and developer by passion, Developer Relation @ Google by profession
Leading the Google Developer Experts (GDE) program - Deep knowledge in a Google tech? Love to share your knowledge with other? We need to talk!

I relentlessly train myself in preparing (and eating) Tiramisù
I'm definitely yellow 💛

data.title: The Evolution of the Agentic OS
data.sentence: From 'question to answer' to 'goal to result'

data.title: The Paradigm Shift
bullets: "Simple" chat applications
data.speaker_notes: ChatGPT came on 2022. Impacful, but you had to craft "The Perfect Prompt" to ri-elaborate information. Limited because it could only talk back to you, and could not sent emails, browse and interact with sites, you need to pass the files, etc. The app wrote the email draft, but you had to copy and past it.

data.title: The Paradigm Shift
bullets: "Simple" chat applications
Reasoning, more advanced LLMs, RAG, sub-agents, etc
data.speaker_notes: Then, over time, LLMs became more capable, added reasoning, added a bigger context windows, RAG to retrieve information in a smart way, and much more. But it was still a chat application.

data.title: The Paradigm Shift
bullets: "Simple" chat applications
Reasoning, more advanced LLMs, RAG, sub-agents, etc
Tools
data.speaker_notes: Finally, there agents started to have the possibility to interact with the rest of the world. They got the first function calling capabilities, AKA tools. Agents finally had the ability to act.

data.title: The Paradigm Shift
bullets: "Simple" chat applications
Reasoning, more advanced LLMs, RAG, sub-agents, etc
Tools, MCP, AgentSkill
data.speaker_notes: And from tools, MCP emerged to give even more capabilities to agent, using a common format. And them we got AgentSkills, as a way to packages and repeat specific flows.

data.title: The Paradigm Shift
bullets: "Simple" chat applications
Reasoning, more advanced LLMs, RAG, sub-agents, etc
Tools, MCP, AgentSkill -> Harness
data.speaker_notes:
All of this layer around the LLM created the harness. It's like the "entire car" built around the engine. The engine, without the car, goes nowhere. The car, without the engine, cannot move.
But together, they work. They can move!

data.title: The Paradigm Shift
bullets: "Simple" chat applications
Reasoning, more advanced LLMs, RAG, sub-agents, etc
Tools, MCP, AgentSkill -> Harness -> Agentic Workflows
data.speaker_notes: And thanks to all of this, we have now our Agentic Workflows. Entire flows solved using LLMs and they harnesses. By their own, without our interventions.

data.title: The Paradigm Shift
bullets: "Simple" chat applications
Reasoning, more advanced LLMs, RAG, sub-agents, etc
Tools, MCP, AgentSkill -> Harness -> Agentic Workflows
🦞 OpenClaw
data.speaker_notes: And then, OpenClaw arrived.

data.title: 🦞 OpenClaw
bullets: The harness that gave the AI brain a body
data.speaker_notes: OpenClaw is an agent, configured with a particular harness. And with a application layer to access them.

First PERSONAL and AUTONOMOUS agent
data.speaker_notes: OpenClaw is the first PERSONAL and AUTONOMOUS agent

data.title: The Paradigm Shift
bullets: "Simple" chat applications
Reasoning, more advanced LLMs, RAG, sub-agents, etc
Tools, MCP, AgentSkill -> Harness -> Agentic Workflows
🦞 OpenClaw
Agentic OS
data.speaker_notes:
OpenClaw paved the way for the Agentic OS. The Agentic OS is a software layer that manages and coordinates autonomous AI agents to do tasks for you, rather than offering applications to interact with to the job done.
A traditional operating system takes instructions. An AI operating system takes objectives.
It enables AI to plan, take action, and execute complex workflows independently—such as sending emails or scheduling tasks—by interacting with software, files, and tools on the user's behalf.

data.title: What is OpenClaw?
data.sentence: The harness that gives the AI brain a body.
data.speaker_notes: So, what exactly is OpenClaw? To understand it, we must break down the internal mechanism an agent uses to achieve its goals, which is known as the Agent Loop: Observe, Think, and Act.

data.quote: The agent is only as good as the system you build around it
data.attribution: And OpenClaw gives you the tool to control all of this
data.speaker_notes: Thank you for your time. I'd now like to open the floor to any questions you might have.

bullets: Observe: Gather context and research
Think: Formulate a plan for the next step
Act: Write code or execute tools

slides.speaker_notes: When you give the agent a prompt, it doesn't just generate text; it continuously loops through three steps. First, it Observes by checking the workspace and gathering context. Second, it Thinks about what to do next and formulates a plan. Finally, it Acts by writing code or using tools.

data.title: The Core Mechanics

bullets: Observe: Gather context and research
Think: Formulate a plan for the next step
Act: Write code or execute tools
LLM serves as the brain (Claude, GPT, Gemini, local models)
OpenClaw serves as the harness (hands, eyes, skills)

data.speaker_notes: A helpful way to visualize this is the 'Brain in the Middle' model. The LLM acts as the 'brain,' but OpenClaw acts as the 'harness' or the body surrounding the brain, giving it hands, eyes, and an ecosystem of skills.

data.title: The OpenClaw Harness
data.speaker_notes:
3 layers-system.
- Layer 1: 8 Core tools like filesystem access, internet search and fetch, execute local commands.
- Layer 2: Advances tools to manage sessions, memory, cron, additional nodes to connect to OpenClaw
- Layer 3: Built-in Skills to access to a baseline of services.
Source: https://yu-wenhao.com/en/blog/openclaw-tools-skills-tutorial/

data.title: Interaction Layers & Tooling
bullets: Tools: Typed functions for taking action
Skills: Markdown SOPs (Standard Operative Procedures) for step-by-step processes, in AgentSkill format
Plugins: Community packages for models and integrations
MCP client: OpenClaw can act as MCP client to multiple MCP servers
data.speaker_notes: OpenClaw interacts with the world using three primary layers: Tools, Skills, and Plugins. Tools are the typed functions the agent invokes to take action. Skills are Markdown files injected into the system prompt that act as SOPs (Standard Operative Procedures). They are defined using the AgentSkill standard. Finally, plugins are packages published by the community.

data.title: Identity Files
bullets: AGENTS.md: Operating instructions for the agent and how it should use memory
SOUL.md: Defines personality, core truths, values, and boundaries
USERS.md: Who the user is and how to address them
IDENTITY.md: Defines the agent's name, vibe, and emojis
TOOLS.md: Notes about your tools and conventions
HEARTBEAT: Optional tiny checklist for heartbeat runs
MEMORY.md: Curated long-term memory
data.speaker_notes:
We do this using Identity Files, which define the agent. SOUL.md defines the agent's personality, core truths, values, and boundaries. IDENTITY.md is a minimal file defining who the agent is. AGENTS.md acts as your agent's Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) rulebook. TOOLS.md is a sticky note that reminds the agent of all the tools it has handy at its disposal.
Source: https://docs.openclaw.ai/concepts/agent-workspace

data.title: Memory and Context
bullets: MEMORY.md: Curated long-term memory
Daily Memory: Synthesizes important facts into a daily memory.md file
Context Compaction: Automatically compacts older conversations to save space
Segmentation: Use separate channels/threads for distinct tasks
Vector Search and RAG (locally or on a second brain like Obsidial Vaults)
data.speaker_notes: Managing memory and context is a critical aspect of an Agentic OS. OpenClaw automatically and continuously compacts older conversations to save space. Every night, it creates a daily memory.md file. A crucial best practice for memory management is segmenting your conversations into different channels. OpenClaw is also highly reactive thanks to its 'heartbeat', waking up every 30 minutes to proactively check tasks. Finally, you can elevate your agent's memory by integrating an Obsidian Vault for Vector Memory Search and RAG.

data.title: Proactivity
bullets: Heartbeat: Internal cron job wakes up every 30 minutes to run background workflows
Cron Scheduler: Execute specific jobs at specific times
data.speaker_notes: OpenClaw is also highly reactive thanks to its 'heartbeat', waking up every 30 minutes to proactively check tasks. It also integrates a cron scheduler, to execute specific jobs at specific times. Ant these jobs are prompt sent to the agent. They retain the identity of the agent (but not the memory)

data.title: Interaction surfaces
bullets: OpenClaw TUI and GUI as a baseline interaction surface
Seamless interaction via WhatsApp, Discord, Slack, Telegram, Signal, Teams, etc, etc...
Supports asynchronous voice memos
OpenClaw Gateway interact via CLI
OpenClaw MCP Server
data.speaker_notes: Furthermore, OpenClaw introduces seamless interaction through user-friendly channels. Instead of just typing commands into a terminal, you can interact with your agent using the chat apps you already use every day. You can even send it voice memos while you are driving.

Teaching agents

data.title: How to "Install" OpenClaw
data.sentence: Choosing your hardware, making your magic, and reducing the blast radius.
data.speaker_notes: Since our time is limited today, I will skip the basic step-by-step installation tutorial and jump straight into hardware choices, security advice, and how to configure an active system.

data.title: Hardware Choices

bullets: Local computer: Local execution for top-tier privacy
Headless vs desktop setup: Access to camera and other OS-specific skills
Local inference: run LLMs locally, do not use cloud models
VPS: Always-on, isolated
Raspberry Pi: even a RasPi4, 2GB of RAM is enough for running OpenClaw

data.speaker_notes: When it comes to hardware, you have a few choices. You can run OpenClaw locally on a dedicated Mac Mini... Alternatively, you can run it on a VPS... No matter what hardware you choose, you must immediately implement core security practices to reduce the 'blast radius' if something goes wrong. The most crucial rule is to never run OpenClaw using a root or admin account. Always apply the principle of least privilege.

data.title: Hardware Choices & Security

bullets: Local computer: Local execution for top-tier privacy
Headless vs desktop setup: Access to camera and other OS-specific skills
Local inference: run LLMs locally, do not use cloud models
VPS: Always-on, isolated
Raspberry Pi: even a RasPi4, 2GB of RAM is enough for running OpenClaw
Never run with root or admin accounts
Sandbox environments using Docker or restricted users
Never expose the Gateway on the internet
Apply the principle of least privilege

data.speaker_notes: No matter what hardware you choose, you must immediately implement core security practices to reduce the 'blast radius' if something goes wrong. The most crucial rule is to never run OpenClaw using a root or admin account. Sandboxing is hard, but it pays you back. Always apply the principle of least privilege.

data.title: Creating your first agent
bullets: Hatch the agent as onboarding a real human
data.speaker_notes: Once installed, setting up OpenClaw is exactly like onboarding a real human employee. You cannot expect an executive assistant to walk into your office on day one and flawlessly execute tasks without understanding your business, your clients, and your tools. You must provide it with business context.

data.title: Creating your first agent
bullets: Hatch the agent as onboarding a real human
Configure chaneels (_optional, but useful_)
data.speaker_notes: Select how you want to interact with your agent. Planning to use with other family members? Maybe Whatsapp is the right approach. Want flexibility and multi-agent system? Discord backs you up. Wanna start playing as soon as possible, and locally? Just use the GUI.

data.title: Creating your first agent
bullets: Hatch the agent as onboarding a real human
Configure chaneels (_optional, but useful_)
Start building your first workflow
data.speaker_notes: When building your Agentic OS, you must start simple. Start with a single agent handling a basic task. Move away from unstructured 'vibe coding' toward defining strict, repeatable, and logical processes.

data.title: The SWIFT Framework
bullets: Setup: Scope the project and connect API keys/MCPs
Workflow: Build the first draft manually with the agent
Iterate: Refine the output and prompt instructions
Formalize: Turn the perfected process into a repeatable .md skill
Trigger: Set to a cron schedule for automatic background execution
data.speaker_notes: Once you perfect a manual workflow in chat, move that workflow into a Skill. To systematically achieve this, we use the SWIFT Framework: Setup, Workflow, Iterate, Formalize, and Trigger. This turns perfected processes into repeatable skills and triggers them with cron schedules.

data.title: Mitigation Strategies
bullets: Every surface can be a vector for prompt injection (emails, web pages, messages, etc)
Defensive text sanitation layers and frontier model scanning
data.speaker_notes: As we integrate our agents with the real world, security is paramount. You must understand that every input channel is a potential prompt injection surface. You must establish defensive text sanitation layers.

data.title: Mitigation Strategies
bullets: Every surface can be a vector for prompt injection (emails, web pages, messages, etc)
Defensive text sanitation layers and frontier model scanning
Separate identities: enthusiasm can be dangerous!
Read-only access limits and draft approvals
data.speaker_notes: Second, you must implement separate identities to reduce the blast radius. Do not let the agent operate using your personal browser profile. Create a distinct entity. Never blindly hand over the 'Keys to the Kingdom'. Limit the agent to read-only access wherever possible, verifying its work before approving destructive actions.

data.title: Mitigation Strategies
bullets: Every surface can be a vector for prompt injection (emails, web pages, messages, etc)
Defensive text sanitation layers and frontier model scanning
Separate identities: enthusiasm can be dangerous!
Read-only access limits and draft approvals
Malicious supply chain code in skills
Source code auditing before installing skills
data.speaker_notes: Finally, always audit the source code of a third-party skill before installing it.

data.title: Auto-Improving the OS
bullets: LLM reviews codebase and logs daily
Identifies inefficiencies and proposes changes
Diagnoses and repairs broken workflows
Maintains the 'Human in the Loop' principle
data.speaker_notes: Your Agentic OS can also auto-improve. By leveraging the generative aspect of the LLM, you can tell OpenClaw to review its own codebase and conversation logs daily. It will analyze how a task was handled, identify inefficiencies, and propose code changes. And remember: shit happens. Keep a 'human in the loop,' but ask OpenClaw to solve its own problems by diagnosing log errors.

data.title: A close look at the future
data.sentence: Which is probably the next 4 weeks... 🤯
data.speaker_notes: To wrap up, the ecosystem around Agentic AI is moving at a breakneck pace. OpenClaw is phenomenal, but it exists in a rapidly evolving landscape.

data.title: The Future: AI Swarms
bullets: Transitioning from a single-agent framework to a true Agentic Operating System
Orchestrating swarms of self-coordinating agents
Specialized agents handling marketing, coding, and finance simultaneously
The user acts as the human conductor of an entire AI workforce
data.speaker_notes: The true future we are building toward is Swarms. OpenClaw is transitioning from a framework that runs a single AI agent into a true Operating System that orchestrates entire swarms of self-coordinating agents. Soon, you won't just have an assistant; you will have an entire AI workforce reporting back to you, the human conductor. OpenClaw is becoming the Agentic OS.

data.title: A New Engineering Discipline

bullets: Crafting the perfect prompt
Manual execution of tasks
Iterative Q&A refinement
Building the cognitive infrastructure for a fleet of agents
Context engineering for rich backgrounds
Incredibly simple daily prompts
Architecting and guiding automated systems

data.speaker_notes: This shift requires a new engineering discipline. We are moving away from traditional 'prompt engineering' and entering the era of 'agentic engineering'. In the past, people spent hours crafting the perfect prompt to get the right output. With an Agentic OS, you focus on 'context engineering,' where you load your agent with robust context about yourself, your business, and your preferences. Your daily job as a developer or operator changes from executing manual digital tasks to architecting and guiding these automated systems.
